title: Java Serialization
description: Serialize an object graph to bytes with ObjectOutputStream.
learningGoals: Mark a type Serializable so its instances can be written to a stream.
Write an object to bytes with ObjectOutputStream.writeObject.

completionChecks.prompt: What does implements Serializable provide here?
choices: A guarantee that every field is encrypted
A marker that Account may be serialized
completionChecks.explanation: Serializable is a marker interface; it marks the type as eligible for Java object serialization.

completionChecks.prompt: Which call actually writes the account object?
completionChecks.explanation: ObjectOutputStream.writeObject serializes the object graph into the underlying byte stream.

completionChecks.prompt: What happens when save(new Account("a", 10)) runs without an IOException?
choices: It returns a nonempty byte array containing serialized object data
It returns an empty byte array because records have no fields to serialize
It returns the UTF-8 text a:10
It throws NotSerializableException because records cannot implement Serializable
completionChecks.explanation: A record may implement Serializable, and ObjectOutputStream writes binary serialization data into the byte array.
